message:
🌱 Add flagger: Switching between canary and k8s rollout with no downtime mission (#746)

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,77 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "flagger-388-switching-between-canary-and-k8s-rollout-with-no-downtime",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "flagger: Switching between canary and k8s rollout with no downtime",
+    "description": "Switching between canary and k8s rollout with no downtime. Community-requested feature.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current flagger deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your flagger version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n flagger -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flagger\nhelm list -n flagger 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working flagger installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review flagger configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant flagger configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n flagger -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flagger\nkubectl get configmap -n flagger -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=flagger\n```\nIs it possible to switch between canary and k8s rollout with no downtime?\n\nCurrently, when I delete Canary object, my original deployment is left with 0 replicas.\nI use Helm for deployment and Linkerd as service mesh"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Switching between canary and k8s rollout with no downtime",
+        "description": "This is a work in progress PR looking for acceptance on the approach and feedback.  This PR provides the opt-in capability for users to revert flagger mutations on deletion of a canary.  If users opt-in finalizers will be utilized to revert the mutated resources before the canary and owned resources are handed over for finalizing.\n\nChanges:\nAdd evaluations for finalizers controller/controller\nAdd finalizers source controller/finalizer\nAdd interface method on deployment and daemonset\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n flagger -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flagger\nkubectl get events -n flagger --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Switching between canary and k8s rollout with no downtime\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "This is a work in progress PR looking for acceptance on the approach and feedback.  This PR provides the opt-in capability for users to revert flagger mutations on deletion of a canary.  If users opt-in finalizers will be utilized to revert the mutated resources before the canary and owned resources are handed over for finalizing.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {

+      "flagger",
+      "graduated",

+      "feature"

+      "flagger"
+    ],

+      "Deployment",

+      "Daemonset"
+    ],

+    },

+  },
